title: Symmetrical probe for measuring pelvic floor muscles parameters
abstract: A probe for measuring the strength of the pelvic floor muscle to be inserted inside the pelvic cavity of a user comprises an outer shell comprising at least a first layer of biocompatible material, a sensor part comprising a plurality of sensors elements, and a processing unit configured to determined one or more pelvic muscle parameters. A middle cross section of the sensor region has a smaller diameter than cross sections of those regions of the sensor region between which said middle cross section is postioned.
claims: Claims ims A probe for measuring the strength of the pelvic floor muscle when inserted inside the pelvic cavity of a user, the probe extending from a first end to a second end, wherein the probe comprises: â¢ an outer shell comprising at least a first layer of biocompatible material, â¢ a sensor part comprising a plurality of sensors elements, and â¢ a processing unit configured to determined one or more pelvic muscle parameters, wherein the probe further comprises: - a first region at the first end of the probe, - a second region at the second end of the probe, and - a sensor region between the first region and the second region, the sensor region extending on both sides of the pelvic floor muscle of the user when the probe is inserted inside the user, and the sensor region comprising the plurality of sensors elements positioned inside the outer shell of the probe, wherein the sensor region has a plurality of anatomic compliance cross sections each along an axis extending from the first to the second end of the probe, the plurality of cross sections including at least: - a first sensor region cross section having a first sensor region diameter D3,1 , - a second region sensor cross section having a second sensor region diameter D3,2, and - a third sensor region cross section having a third sensor region diameter D3,3, wherein the second sensor region cross section is positioned between the first sensor region cross section and the third sensor region cross section, and wherein D3,1 > D3,2 < D3,3. 2. Probe according to claim 1 , wherein the anatomic compliance cross sections includes circular cross sections, oval cross sections, and polygonial cross sections. 3. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the anatomic compliance cross sections are circular cross sections. 4. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein each cross section extending along the axis from the first end of the probe to the second end of the probe has a circularly shaped outer shell. 5. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first region comprising the processing unit enclosed by the outer shell. 6. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the second region comprising a first antenna enclosed by the outer shell. 7. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a plurality of cross sections each extends along the axis from the first end of the probe to the second end of the probe, the plurality of cross sections including at least: - a first region cross section having a diameter D1 , - a second region cross section having a diameter D2, and - a sensor region cross section having a diameter D3, wherein D1/D3 > 1 .2 and D2/D3 > 1.2. 8. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the sensor elements are positioned next to each other forming a circle with the sensor elements extending in parallel with the axis from the first end of the probe to the second end of the probe. 9. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein two successive sensor elements are positioned at a distance from each other being substantially the same for any two successive sensor elements. 10. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the probe is solid. 1 1 . Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the processing unit has an update rate of a digital input of between 50-500 updates per second, or between 50-200 updates per second, or 75-125 updates per second. 12. Probe according to any of the preceding claims, wherein an individual update will be a running average of the last n measured values, with n being an integer from 1 to 100.
